children.heading: .—General Violations
children.label: a .—General Violations
children.text: In connection with an investigation under section 41301 or 41302 of this title, the Federal Maritime Commission may bring a civil action to enjoin conduct in violation of this part. The action must be brought in the district court of the United States for any judicial district in which the defendant resides or transacts business. After notice to the defendant, and a showing that the standards for granting injunctive relief by courts of equity are met, the court may grant a temporary restraining order or preliminary injunction for a period not to exceed 10 days after the Commission has issued an order disposing of the issues under investigation.

children.heading: .—Action by commission
children.label: 1 .—Action by commission
children.text: If, at any time after the filing or effective date of an agreement under chapter 403 of this title, the Commission determines that the agreement is likely, by a reduction in competition, to produce an unreasonable reduction in transportation service or an unreasonable increase in transportation cost or to substantially lessen competition in the purchasing of certain covered services, the Commission, after notice to the person filing the agreement, may bring a civil action in the United States District Court for the District of Columbia to enjoin the operation of the agreement. The Commission’s sole remedy with respect to an agreement likely to have such an effect is an action under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: a temporary restraining order or a preliminary injunction; and

children.label: B
children.text: a permanent injunction after a showing that the agreement is likely to have the effect described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Burden of proof and third parties
children.label: 3 .—Burden of proof and third parties
children.text: In an action under this subsection, the burden of proof is on the Commission. The court may not allow a third party to intervene.

children.heading: .—Competition factors
children.label: 4 .—Competition factors
children.text: In making a determination under this subsection regarding whether an agreement is likely to substantially lessen competition in the purchasing of certain covered services, the Commission may consider any relevant competition factors in affected markets, including, without limitation, the competitive effect of agreements other than the agreement under review.
shall extend the period specified in until there has been substantial compliance; andsection 40304(c)(2) of this title
may grant other equitable relief that the court decides is appropriate.
a district court of the United States, on notice to the Attorney General; and
a court of appeals of the United States, with the approval of the Attorney General.
